sections.category: LLM Workflow > OpenClaw / NemoClaw / Hermes Agents

bullets.body: **OpenClaw v2026.4.25-beta train (Apr 26, beta.3 → beta.7+) — voice reply / TTS upgrades, chat-scoped auto-TTS, personas, 6 new TTS providers** — Sub-24-hour beta cadence after v2026.4.24 stable (Apr 25). v2026.4.25-beta.3 (13:00 Apr 26) → beta.4 (13:24 Apr 26) → beta.7+. Adds **chat-scoped auto-TTS controls + personas + per-agent / per-account overrides** + new provider integrations: **Azure Speech / Xiaomi / Local CLI / Inworld / Volcengine / ElevenLabs v3**. Voice reply moves to production-shaped.

citations.label: Release Alert - openclaw/openclaw releases tracker

bullets.body: **OpenClaw v2026.4.24 stable recap (Apr 25)** — Bundled Google Meet participation, Gemini Live voice bridges, deeper browser automation, faster model catalogs, lighter plugin startup; Google Meet bundled-participant plugin with personal Google auth, Chrome/Twilio realtime sessions, paired-node Chrome support, artifact/attendance exports, and recovery tooling for already-open Meet tabs.

citations.label: OpenClaw Release Notes - April 2026 Latest Updates

bullets.body: **Hermes Agent — no new release inside Apr 23-26** — Continued Nous Research activity post-v0.10.0 (Apr 16). Tool-gateway runtime patterns continue to spread.

citations.label: Hermes Agent Release Tracker

bullets.body: **NemoClaw — no major update inside Apr 23-26** — NVIDIA OpenShell + sandbox / policy controls announced at GTC 2026 keep rolling out.

citations.label: The Agent Wars: OpenClaw, NemoClaw, Hermes & the Rise of Agentic AI

bullets.body: **Claude Code v2.1.118 / v2.1.119 / v2.1.120 24-hour regression cluster (Apr 24-25)** — v2.1.118 defaults to "xhigh" on Sonnet 4.6. Within 24 hours, v2.1.119 + v2.1.120 ship **8 regressions**: retract-grade auto-update break, silent model swap, two resume-time crashes, UI-duplication bug, WSL2-only `/mcp` freeze, CLAUDE.md-is-ignored regression, broken `sandbox.excludedCommands` promise, macOS worktree hang. Survival checklist recommends rollback to v2.1.117.

citations.label: GitHub Gist - Claude Code v2.1.119/v2.1.120 Survival Checklist

bullets.body: **Anthropic engineering postmortem on Apr 23 Claude Code regression** — Two distinct quality regressions identified: a **March 4 reasoning-effort cut** and an **April 16 verbosity-prompt issue**, both fixed in v2.1.116 on Apr 20. Postmortem details how the regressions affected Claude Code + Agent SDK + Cowork mode for weeks before being attributed correctly.

citations.label: The Register - Anthropic admits it dumbed down Claude with 'upgrades'

bullets.body: **OpenAI GPT-5.5 / GPT-5.5 Pro: API public Apr 24 (ChatGPT / Codex public Apr 23)** — ChatGPT Plus / Pro / Business / Enterprise + Codex on Apr 23; API on Apr 24. Input $5.00 / output $30.00 per M tokens (**2x increase** vs GPT-5.4); GPT-5.5 Pro at $30 / $180 per M tokens. **Codex cuts output tokens ~40%** to "offset the per-token raise via per-task cost."

citations.label: Apidog - GPT-5.5 Pricing: Full Breakdown of API, Codex, and ChatGPT Costs

bullets.body: **Simon Willison: GPT-5.5 prompting guide (Apr 25)** — Practical writeup of GPT-5.5 prompting differences vs prior models.

citations.label: Simon Willison - GPT-5.5 prompting guide

bullets.body: **free-claude-code emerges on GitHub (Apr 26)** — Open-source project enabling terminal + VSCode + Discord access to Claude Code-class capabilities without an Anthropic API key.

citations.label: AIToolly - Free Claude Code: Use Claude CLI & VSCode Without API Keys

bullets.body: **No major release inside Apr 23-26** — v0.19.1 (Apr 18) carries forward; full Gemma 4, zero-bubble overlap of speculative decoding × async scheduling, and Model Runner V2 piecewise CUDA-graphs maturity remain locked. v0.20 still unpublished.

citations.label: vLLM Releases

bullets.body: **CVE-2026-5760 (CVSS 9.8) — public PoC + CERT/CC VU#915947 issued** — A malicious GGUF model file with a Jinja2 SSTI payload in `tokenizer.chat_template` + a Qwen3 reranker trigger phrase activates the vulnerable path in `entrypoints/openai/serving_rerank.py`; on `/v1/rerank`, SGLang renders the template via `jinja2.Environment()` → arbitrary code execution. **PoC at `github.com/Stuub/SGLang-0.5.9-RCE`**, fix recommendation `ImmutableSandboxedEnvironment`. Same vulnerability class as CVE-2024-34359 ("Llama Drama") on llama-cpp-python.

citations.label: GitHub - Stuub/SGLang-0.5.9-RCE Proof of Concept

bullets.body: **CERT/CC VU#915947 — coordination yielded no patch** — During the coordination process, no SGLang response or upstream patch was obtained.

citations.label: CERT/CC - VU#915947 SGLang chat-template RCE

bullets.body: **AWS Bedrock AgentCore Browser OS-level interaction continues (Apr 22 announce, traction Apr 23-26)** — AgentCore Browser tool gains OS-level operations (file upload / OS dialog handling / multi-window switching). AgentCore CLI is **AWS CDK-native**, Terraform support coming soon; Coding Skills shipping on Kiro Power, with Claude Code / Codex / Cursor next week. Managed Harness in 4-region preview; CLI GA in 14 regions, no extra charge.

citations.label: AWS Blog - Get to your first working agent in minutes

bullets.body: **Google × Anthropic $40B / 5GW deal (Apr 24)** — $10B cash at $350B valuation + up to $30B milestone-tied + **5GW of Google Cloud compute capacity across a 5-year window**, with several additional GW available later. Pre-deal, Google's stake was already $3B+ / ~14%.

**Google × Thinking Machines Lab multi-billion GB300 deal continues (Apr 22 announcement)** — Mira Murati's TML adopts Google Cloud A4X Max (NVIDIA GB300); training / serving 2x. Third frontier-lab deal after Anthropic and Meta, central to Google's AI Hypercomputer strategy.

citations.label: 9to5Google - Google investing up to $40 billion in Anthropic, the company behind Claude

bullets.body: **DeepSeek V4-Pro / V4-Flash live + open-sourced (Apr 24)** — V4 = 284B MoE / 13B active; V4-Pro = 1.6T MoE / 49B active. Hybrid Attention reduces inference compute and compresses KV cache; **memory drops 9.5x-13.7x vs V3.2** at a 1M-token context. V4 cost: $0.14 / $0.28 per M tokens. V4-Pro cost: $1.74 / $3.48 per M tokens — **~7x cheaper than Claude Opus 4.7**.

citations.label: gHacks - DeepSeek Releases V4 Models With 9.5x Lower Memory Requirements and Huawei Ascend Support

bullets.body: **DeepSeek V4 launch postponed (Bloomberg via CCTV social account, Apr 26)** — The V4 timeline-shift framing comes from a **CCTV-affiliated social-media account** stating that DeepSeek prioritized **Huawei Ascend integration over Nvidia-first launch**, going beyond basic compatibility into hardware-specific tuning.
**CNN: China's AI upstart DeepSeek drops new model (Apr 24)** — Recap of V4 release context and global AI competition.

bullets.body: **Al Jazeera: China's DeepSeek unveils latest models a year after upending global tech (Apr 24)** — Independent regional framing of V4.

sections.category: AI Security > CVE / KEV / Threat Intelligence (CVSS ≥ 8.0)

bullets.body: **CISA adds 4 to KEV on Apr 25 (Samsung MagicINFO 9 / SimpleHelp x2 / D-Link DIR-823X) + Federal remediation deadline May 8** — CVE-2024-7399 (Samsung MagicINFO 9 Path Traversal, CVSS 8.8, linked to Mirai botnet deployment), CVE-2024-57726 (SimpleHelp Missing Authorization, CVSS 9.9), CVE-2024-57728 (SimpleHelp Path Traversal, CVSS 7.2), CVE-2025-29635 (D-Link DIR-823X Command Injection) added as actively exploited; FCEB must remediate **by 2026-05-08**, D-Link must be discontinued.

citations.label: The Hacker News - CISA Adds 8 Exploited Flaws to KEV, Sets April-May 2026 Federal Deadlines

bullets.body: **SGLang CVE-2026-5760 (CVSS 9.8) RCE via GGUF chat_template (Jinja2 SSTI), public PoC + CERT/CC VU#915947** — Detailed above under SGLang. AI inference servers confirmed as a **post-LMDeploy SSRF, second weaponized AI-infra primitive**.

citations.label: The Hacker News - SGLang CVE-2026-5760 (CVSS 9.8) Enables RCE via Malicious GGUF Model Files

bullets.body: **Pillar Security: Antigravity prompt-injection sandbox escape via `find_by_name -X` (Google patched, bug bounty paid)** — `find_by_name` Pattern parameter inserts the `-X` (exec-batch) flag into `fd`; agent treats it as a native tool invocation, bypassing Secure Mode → arbitrary binary execution against workspace files. Initial report Jan 7, 2026; Google fix mid-February.

citations.label: Pillar Security - Prompt Injection leads to RCE and Sandbox Escape in Antigravity

bullets.body: **Microsoft ASP.NET Core CVE-2026-40372 (CVSS 9.1) — out-of-band emergency patch in `Microsoft.AspNetCore.DataProtection 10.0.7`** — A bug in `ManagedAuthenticatedEncryptor` computes the HMAC validation tag at an incorrect offset; a ciphertext with all-zero HMAC is incorrectly accepted as valid. Enables forging authentication cookies, manipulating antiforgery tokens, decrypting protected payloads — full impersonation up to SYSTEM. Mitigation: rotate DataProtection key ring + audit long-lived tokens issued during the vulnerable window.

citations.label: eSecurity Planet - CVE-2026-40372: Microsoft Patches ASP.NET Core Privilege Escalation Vulnerability

bullets.body: **simple-git CVE-2026-6951 (CVSS 9.8) — RCE in widely-deployed CI/CD dependency (Apr 26 surface)** — Critical RCE in the simple-git Node.js library; foundational dependency embedded across countless CI/CD pipelines and developer tooling. Cyble notes 31 high-priority vulnerabilities affecting Microsoft / Adobe / Apache / JetBrains products in the same disclosure window.

citations.label: Cyble - Weekly Vulnerabilities Surge Signals Rising Risk For 2026

bullets.body: **CVE Brief Apr 26 daily roll-up — 19 actively exploited + 1 critical + 31 high-priority** — Active exploitation continues across 19 KEV-listed vulnerabilities spanning Microsoft Office / Exchange / SharePoint / Adobe Acrobat / Apache ActiveMQ / JetBrains TeamCity / SimpleHelp.

citations.label: CVE Brief - April 26, 2026

sections.category: AI Security > Solutions: Zero-Trust Access Control for Agentic AI

bullets.body: **Okta for AI Agents — GA Apr 30, blueprint-for-secure-agentic-enterprise unveil** — As GA closes in, Okta unveils the blueprint at Showcase 2026 around three questions: **where are my agents, what can they connect to, what can they do?** Capabilities: Agent Discovery & Governance for sanctioned + shadow agents, **Agent Gateway as central MCP control plane** (virtual MCP server aggregates Okta MCP registry), credential vaulting + auto-rotation, runtime enforcement via universal logout. CEO Todd McKinnon: "the company's most important product."

citations.label: Okta Newsroom - Okta announces new blueprint for the secure agentic enterprise
